proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across smoking status categories of feature_001 (current vs former vs never).
Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with feature_006='squamous' than in patients with feature_006='adenocarcinoma'.
Mean pfs_months is monotonically lower as feature_014 increases from level 0 to level 2 (likely a stage-/ECOG-like ordinal severity variable).

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across feature_001: F=433.0, p=3.65e-187. Means: former=3.61, current=2.99, never=3.68 — current smokers have markedly shorter PFS.
Mean PFS adenocarcinoma=3.67 vs squamous=2.81; squamous-vs-adeno difference=-0.86 months, p<1e-300.
Mean PFS by feature_014: 0=4.33, 1=3.18, 2=2.15. Strong monotone decrease, ANOVA F=3141, p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90 — likely age in years) is positively correlated with pfs_months.

Continuous lab-like variables feature_025 (range 1.7-5.5, possibly albumin) and feature_017 (range 0-28, possibly an inflammation marker) are correlated with pfs_months — feature_025 positively, feature_017 negatively.
feature_019 (continuous, 0-0.798 — possibly PD-L1 fraction) has no main effect on pfs_months when adjusted for other covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(feature_015, pfs_months) = +0.757, p<1e-300. Each year higher in feature_015 corresponds to ~0.17 month longer PFS in adjusted model.
feature_025 r=+0.099 (p=2.8e-109); feature_017 r=-0.127 (p=7.5e-179). Both directions match hypothesis.
In adjusted multivariable OLS, feature_019 coef=+0.007, p=0.67 — no detectable main effect, consistent with PD-L1 only mattering conditional on therapy class.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS of pfs_months on all baseline features explains most variance (R^2 > 0.5), with feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_006, feature_016, feature_017, feature_025, feature_001 as the dominant terms.
Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with feature_023=1 than with feature_023=0 (a high-prevalence binary prognostic indicator).
Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with feature_011=1 than with feature_011=0, and the negative effect is uniform across clinically relevant strata (smoking, histology, feature_014).
Mean pfs_months is HIGHER in patients with feature_016=1 than with feature_016=0 (a positive prognostic/predictive marker).

analyses.result_summary: feature_023=1 mean=2.93 vs =0 mean=4.36, diff=-1.44 months, p<1e-300. Strongly confirms negative prognostic.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of feature_011 within histology, smoking, age tertile, feature_014
analyses.result_summary: feature_011=1 vs =0: -0.92 months overall, p<1e-300. Strikingly uniform: adeno -0.96, squamous -0.81; current -0.86, former -0.96, never -0.92; feature_014=0 -1.01, =1 -0.91, =2 -0.84; age low -0.76, mid -1.01, high -0.97. Treatment-effect-like uniformity in direction; magnitude near constant.
feature_016=1 mean=4.11 vs =0 mean=3.33, diff=+0.78 months, p=4.8e-151. Positive — but the marginal effect is modest, suggesting a possibly larger conditional effect via interaction with treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 is a randomized treatment-like indicator: it is balanced across all other baseline features (no chi-square/t-test p<1e-3), has prevalence 50%, and has no main effect on pfs_months.

feature_018 is a treatment-like indicator (prevalence 35%, balance with all baselines), with a positive marginal main effect on pfs_months that is largely concentrated in a biomarker-defined subgroup rather than uniformly distributed.

analyses.code: for each other column, chi2/t-test vs feature_012; OLS pfs_months ~ feature_012
analyses.result_summary: feature_012: 1/26 baselines associated at p<0.05 (max -log10p=1.64), perfectly consistent with random assignment. Main-effect t-test on PFS: feature_012=1 mean=3.41 vs =0 mean=3.45, diff=-0.035, p=0.08. Confirms treatment-candidate (no effect).

analyses.code: for each other column, chi2/t-test vs feature_018; OLS pfs_months ~ feature_018
analyses.result_summary: feature_018: only 4/26 baseline associations significant at p<0.05 (max -log10p=3.08), consistent with random assignment. Main-effect t-test: feature_018=1 mean=3.64 vs =0 mean=3.32, diff=+0.32, p=1.6e-53. Modest positive marginal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 has heterogeneous treatment effect (HTE): in an adjusted OLS, feature_018 x feature_016 interaction is large and positive — i.e., feature_018 increases pfs_months much more in feature_016=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_018 x feature_016 interaction coef=+2.533, p<1e-300. Massive predictive interaction. Stratified t-test: feature_016=0 -> feature_018 effect = -0.005 (p=0.80, null); feature_016=1 -> feature_018 effect = +2.55 months (p=2.5e-275). Effect is concentrated in feature_016=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1 patients, the feature_018 treatment effect is further suppressed in patients with feature_031=1 (i.e., feature_031=1 acts as a resistance/non-response indicator that nullifies the treatment effect).

analyses.code: ttest of feature_018 effect within feature_016=1 stratified by feature_031
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1, feature_031=0: feature_018 effect = +4.64 months (treat 7.88 vs ctrl 3.23, p<1e-300, n_treat=1237, n_ctrl=2271). Within feature_016=1, feature_031=1: feature_018 effect = -0.01 (NS, p=0.88). feature_031=1 fully abolishes the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 subgroup, feature_028=1 further suppresses the feature_018 treatment effect (a second resistance modifier).
Within the feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 subgroup, feature_005=1 also further suppresses the feature_018 treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1 & feature_031=0, the C(feature_018)[1]:C(feature_028)[1] interaction coef = -5.117, p=1.4e-35. Among the small (n=135) subset where feature_028=1, feature_018 effect = -0.22 (NS); among feature_028=0 patients (n=3373) treatment effect remains very large.

Within feature_016=1 & feature_031=0, the C(feature_018)[1]:C(feature_005)[1] interaction coef = -4.865, p=8.2e-26. feature_005=1 (n=104) patients show no treatment effect (+0.05); feature_005=0 patients retain the full effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete treatment-effect responder subgroup for feature_018 is defined by ALL of: feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; in this subgroup feature_018 increases pfs_months by ~5 months, and outside this subgroup the effect is null.

analyses.result_summary: C(feature_018)[1]:C(responder)[1] interaction coef = +4.992, p<1e-300. In responder subgroup (n=3266, 6.53% of cohort): treatment 8.23 vs control 3.25, diff=+4.99 months (p<1e-300). In non-responder population: treatment 3.32 vs control 3.32, diff=-0.006 (p=0.76). Each single 'unfavorable' flip of one subgroup variable individually nullifies the effect (all four leave-one-out subgroups give null effects).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_018 treatment effect within the responder subgroup is approximately constant on the additive PFS scale across age (feature_015) tertiles (no further effect modification by age).

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of feature_018 effect within responder subgroup by feature_015 tertile
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, feature_018 absolute treatment effect: low age tertile = +4.73, mid = +4.82, high = +5.05. Quite uniform across age (modest +0.03 month/year additional benefit, p=1.9e-9 — small interaction but not changing the qualitative pattern).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 — though balanced across baselines like a randomized indicator — has no subgroup in which its effect on pfs_months is positive; effect is uniformly negative across all binary, categorical, and ordinal strata.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of feature_011 effect across many subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Across 30+ stratified comparisons by feature_001, feature_006, feature_014, feature_016, feature_018, feature_022, feature_023, feature_028, feature_005, feature_021, feature_002, feature_020, feature_027, feature_012, feature_031 and age tertiles, feature_011=1 always decreases pfs_months by between -0.78 and -1.01 months; never reverses. No predictive HTE detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (50/50 balanced indicator) has no detectable treatment-effect heterogeneity: in adjusted interaction screens with all 32 other features, no interaction is significant after Bonferroni correction.

analyses.code: for each other column c: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ baselines + C(feature_012)*c', data=df).fit() — extract feature_012 interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Smallest feature_012 interaction p across 33 candidate modifiers = 0.003 (with feature_010). No interaction survives Bonferroni 0.05/33 ~ 1.5e-3. feature_012 is consistent with a true null treatment, no HTE.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 (high-prevalence binary, 65% positive) is more like a baseline severity marker than a randomized treatment: its strong negative effect is partially modified by feature_015 (age) and feature_014 (severity), with the largest negative impact in older / higher feature_014 patients.

analyses.result_summary: feature_023 x feature_015 interaction coef = -0.014/year, p=1.3e-89: i.e., feature_023's negative impact grows by an extra 0.014 months per year of age. feature_023 x C(feature_014)[2] = +0.231, p=4.6e-28 (relatively less harmful when feature_014=2 already very advanced). Pattern consistent with baseline severity rather than a randomized treatment with HTE.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_021 is NOT a randomized indicator: it is materially imbalanced with feature_001 (smoking), feature_006, feature_022, feature_016, and feature_028, indicating it is correlated with biology (e.g., a clinical marker or treatment selected non-randomly).

analyses.code: for each c, chi2/t-test of feature_021 vs c
analyses.result_summary: feature_021 imbalances: feature_001 p<1e-300, feature_022 p=2.2e-117, feature_006 p=1.0e-67, feature_016 p=5.3e-25, feature_028 p=2.9e-7. Strongly imbalanced — not a randomized indicator. Adjusted feature_021 main effect ~ 0 (p=0.46) and no interactions survive multiple-testing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_017 (continuous lab-like marker) has a negative association with pfs_months that survives adjustment, and its effect is approximately linear (no strong threshold or interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_017 = -0.073 months per unit, p=7e-179. Direction confirmed; orthogonal to feature_023 and feature_011 (correlations near 0). Likely an inflammation/biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Histology (feature_006=squamous) and smoking status (feature_001=current) each independently shorten pfs_months in adjusted models; the negative effect of current smoking persists after controlling for histology.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted: C(feature_006)[squamous] coef = -0.72, p<1e-300; C(feature_001)[former] vs current = +0.55, never vs current = +0.55 (both p<1e-43). Both effects independent and substantial.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_025 has a positive adjusted association with pfs_months consistent with a beneficial baseline lab (e.g., albumin); each unit increase yields ~0.46 months longer PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_025 coef = +0.463, p<1e-300. Direction consistent with a 'higher = better' lab marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous features feature_032, feature_024, feature_010, feature_003, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009, only feature_032 has a non-trivial adjusted main effect on pfs_months; the others are essentially null.

analyses.code: Inspect adjusted OLS coefficients and p-values for each continuous predictor.
analyses.result_summary: Only feature_032 (coef=-0.000446 per unit, p=1.4e-28) has a clearly non-null adjusted effect among that group. feature_003, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009 all have |coef|<0.01/unit and p>0.05. Net: only feature_032 contributes additional prognostic info beyond the major variables.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Heterogeneity-of-treatment-effect search across all balanced binary candidates (feature_011, feature_012, feature_023, feature_002, feature_005, feature_031, feature_018) finds true predictive HTE only for feature_018.

analyses.code: for each candidate cand in balanced_binaries: top adjusted interaction with each other column
analyses.result_summary: feature_018: top interaction with feature_016 coef=+2.53, p<1e-300 (clean predictive HTE). feature_011: top interaction with feature_015 coef=-0.014, p=6e-78 (continuous gradient, not a clean responder subgroup; uniform direction). feature_012: top interaction p=6e-3 (no surviving HTE). feature_023, feature_005, feature_031, feature_002 all show their top interactions WITH feature_016 / feature_018 — these are reflections of the feature_018 responder subgroup itself rather than independent treatments. Conclusion: feature_018 is the only randomized treatment with predictive HTE in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_018 treatment effect within the full responder subgroup (feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_005=0) is preserved across smoking subgroups feature_001, with all three smoking categories showing >+1.7 month improvement.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of feature_018 effect within feature_016=1 subgroup by feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1: current smokers diff=+2.48 (p=8e-126), former smokers diff=+2.66 (p=1e-152), never smokers diff=+1.76 (p=8e-5). Effect persists in all smoking strata; never smokers show somewhat smaller absolute benefit but still significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for the responder-subgroup interaction, feature_018 has no independent main effect on pfs_months in the rest of the cohort (i.e., feature_018=1 vs =0 within non-responder population yields a null difference).

analyses.result_summary: Non-responders (n=46734): feature_018=1 mean=3.32 vs =0 mean=3.32, diff=-0.006 months, p=0.76. Confirms purely subgroup-restricted effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the rare binary features, feature_022 (13% prev) and feature_028 (5% prev) are NOT randomized treatment indicators: they are imbalanced with multiple baselines and their main effects are consistent with biological subgroup markers.

analyses.code: balance check + stratified PFS for feature_022, feature_028
analyses.result_summary: feature_022: 5 imbalanced baselines, max -log10p=132. feature_028: 6 imbalanced, max -log10p=144. feature_022=1 mean PFS=3.60 vs =0 3.41, p=2e-10 (small positive). feature_028=1 mean=2.66 vs =0 3.47, p=3e-69 (negative). Both behave like biology markers, not randomized treatments. feature_028 in particular acts as a resistance modifier of feature_018 (see h15).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond the feature_018 responder subgroup, no other treatment-by-subgroup heterogeneity rises above multiple-testing thresholds for the feature_011 or feature_012 candidate treatments — i.e., these are uniform-effect (or null) treatments.

analyses.code: exhaustive interaction screen for feature_011 and feature_012 on adjusted PFS
analyses.result_summary: feature_011: strongest interactions are with continuous feature_015 (p=6e-78) and feature_006 squamous (p=1e-13), but they only modulate the magnitude (-0.78 to -1.01) without ever reversing direction — i.e., a quantitative not qualitative HTE pattern. feature_012: nothing surviving multiple testing. No clinically meaningful subgroup where feature_011 helps or feature_012 acts.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint model summary: a parsimonious OLS containing feature_015, C(feature_014), C(feature_023), C(feature_011), C(feature_006), feature_017, feature_025, C(feature_001), and the C(feature_018):responder interaction captures the dominant prognostic and predictive signal in this cohort.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + C(feature_014) + C(feature_023) + C(feature_011) + C(feature_006) + feature_017 + feature_025 + C(feature_001) + C(feature_018)*responder', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full multivariable model achieves R^2 ~ 0.89; the interaction C(feature_018):responder remains highly significant (~+5 months, p<1e-300) while the listed baselines retain their signs and significance. Confirms a simple parsimonious story: dominant prognostic structure (age-like feature_015, severity feature_014, marker feature_023, marker feature_011, histology, lab feature_017/feature_025, smoking) plus one large biomarker-defined treatment effect from feature_018.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL HTE STATEMENT: For pfs_months, the treatment indicator feature_018 has a heterogeneous effect; the complete effect-modifier subgroup is feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0, where mean PFS rises by approximately +5 months on treatment, and outside this subgroup the effect is null. No analogous treatment-effect subgroup is detectable for any other randomized binary feature.

analyses.result_summary: Final 4-cell summary (mean PFS, n): responder=0 & feature_018=0: 3.323 (n=30344); responder=0 & feature_018=1: 3.317 (n=16390); responder=1 & feature_018=0: 3.249 (n=2112); responder=1 & feature_018=1: 8.234 (n=1154). Interaction coef = +4.992 (p<1e-300). Each single 'flip' of one of the four subgroup conditions individually returns the treatment effect to null (all p>0.5). This is the maximally-defined, complete responder subgroup for feature_018.
